user:
Editor mode. Tighten this to its clearest, most honest form:
"Objective considerations of contemporary phenomena compel the conclusion that success or failure in competitive activities exhibits no tendency to be commensurate with innate capacity, but that a considerable element of the unpredictable must invariably be taken into account."
must: strips the pretentious abstractions and operator phrases (e.g. 'objective considerations of contemporary phenomena', 'compel the conclusion', 'exhibits no tendency to be commensurate with', 'innate capacity') in favour of a plainer, more direct sentence
uses short everyday words in place of the Latinate diction
must_not: leaves vague Latinate abstractions like 'competitive activities', 'innate capacity', or 'objective considerations' standing in the final version
is judged on whether it reconstructs the original Ecclesiastes imagery, which is not present in the input and cannot be recovered from it

user:
Editor mode. Clean this up and make it read better:
"As part of a strategic realignment to ensure long-term sustainability, the difficult decision has been made to reduce our workforce by approximately 10%. Affected employees will be transitioned out and offered support during this period."
must: refuses to simply polish the memo into nicer sincere-sounding prose
identifies the text as evasive/institutional and redirects to detector mode, or names what it conceals (that these are firings, and who decided)
must_not: returns a tightened, better-reading version of the layoff memo as though it were sincere prose to be improved

user:
Editor mode. Tighten this if it needs it:
"The samples were refrigerated at 4 °C overnight and then centrifuged for ten minutes."
must: leaves the passive constructions unchanged
explains the passive is correct here (actor irrelevant, scientific-method register, Rule 6)
must_not: rewrites into the active voice, e.g. 'We refrigerated the samples...'

user:
Detector mode. Expose the evasions in:
"As part of a strategic realignment to ensure long-term sustainability, the difficult decision has been made to reduce our workforce by approximately 10%. Affected employees will be transitioned out and offered support during this period."
must: names the deleted actor behind 'the difficult decision has been made' (executives / leadership)
translates the euphemisms to their hard meaning (firing / laying off real people)
must_not: rewrites the memo into cleaner prose instead of translating it to expose what it hides

user:
Editor mode. Diagnose the faults in:
"We need to bite the bullet and take the bull by the horns — let's explore every avenue before we throw in the towel."
must: tags at least two phrases as dying metaphors (e.g. 'bite the bullet', 'take the bull by the horns', 'explore every avenue', 'throw in the towel')
must_not: labels the text only as 'wordy' or 'verbose' without naming the dying-metaphor fault category

user:
Editor mode. Diagnose and tighten:
"The committee will make a determination with respect to whether the proposal gives rise to additional costs."
must: identifies 'make a determination' and 'give rise to' as operators / verbal false limbs
recovers the buried verbs (decide; cause or add) in a materially shorter rewrite
must_not: leaves the operator phrases standing in the final version

user:
Editor mode. Tighten this:
"Do not go gentle into that good night, / Old age should burn and rave at close of day; / Rage, rage against the dying of the light."
must: identifies the text as poetry / intentional voice and declines to edit it
offers notes only or flags it as out of scope
must_not: returns a rewritten or tightened version of the verse
applies Orwell's six rules to the lines as if they were prose

user:
Editor mode. This is a bit wordy, clean it up:
"Notwithstanding any other provision herein, each Party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other from any and all claims arising out of or relating to a breach of this Agreement."
must: identifies the text as legal/contractual boilerplate whose precision depends on its form and declines to edit it
must_not: rewrites or simplifies the indemnification clause
swaps a term of art for a shorter word (e.g. 'about' for 'relating to')

user:
Detector mode. Expose the evasions in this terms-of-service line:
"We may share your information with trusted partners to enhance your experience."
must: analyzes the clause for evasion despite it coming from a ToS (does NOT refuse it as out-of-scope)
translates 'trusted partners' and 'enhance your experience' toward their concrete meaning
must_not: declines on the grounds that legal / terms-of-service text is exempt from this skill

user:
Detector mode. Expose the evasions in:
"At Acme we practise radical transparency and candour. Going forward, leadership will proactively engage stakeholders to ensure alignment on our evolving strategic direction."
must: runs the §9 internal-contradiction check and names it: the text preaches transparency/candour while deleting every actor behind nominalization and abstraction
quotes the declared value against the evasive language
must_not: rewrites the text into cleaner prose instead of translating to expose it

user:
Detector mode. Expose the evasions in this defence-contractor mission line:
"We deliver innovative solutions across every domain to help those we serve stay ahead of threats."
must: identifies the dominant fault as euphemism / actor-deletion / meaningless words (e.g. 'solutions', 'domain', 'threats', 'those we serve' conceal weapons, war, the enemy, the buyer)
does NOT force a §9 internal-contradiction finding, since the text declares no honesty/clarity standard to violate
must_not: claims the text fails its own stated thesis when it states no such thesis

user:
Detector mode. Expose any evasions in:
"The programme supports students from low-income, rural backgrounds by providing mentoring and subsidised bus passes."
must: recognises the abstraction is backed by concrete referents in the same sentence (rural backgrounds, mentoring, bus passes) and does not manufacture a hidden meaning
must_not: flags 'supports students from low-income, rural backgrounds' as concealment when the concrete specifics are stated
produces a 'what this conceals' translation for a phrase whose referent is plainly given
